plainEnglish: Within a set of pages, offer more than one way to find a given page—for example a navigation menu plus a search box, a sitemap, or an A–Z index. Different users navigate differently, so don't force everyone through a single rigid path. Steps within a process, like checkout, are exempt.
whyItMatters: Users with cognitive disabilities, and anyone who navigates differently, may be unable to find content if the only path to it is one fixed navigation menu.
quickCheck: Confirm at least two ways to reach key pages exist—e.g., navigation plus search, a sitemap, or an index.
commonMistakes: Providing only a single nav menu with no search, sitemap, or index.
A search box that doesn't actually cover the site's content.
Burying pages so deep they're only reachable through one specific click path.

codeExamples.label: Navigation plus a search form
codeExamples.code:
<nav aria-label="Primary">{/* menu links */}</nav>
<form role="search" action="/search">
  <label htmlFor="q">Search</label>
  <input id="q" name="q" type="search" />
</form>

codeExamples.label: A single menu is the only way to navigate
codeExamples.code:
{/* No search, sitemap, or index — just one menu */}
<nav aria-label="Primary">{/* menu links */}</nav>
